title: Reliable Bash Automation and Tests · Guided Terminal Studio
summary: Turn a fragile command history into a bounded script that validates inputs, preserves data, reports failures, and can be rerun safely. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a tested idempotent Bash operations script while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
Use set options, traps, explicit conditionals, and status handling while understanding where errexit and pipefail do not behave intuitively.
Handle positional parameters, defaults, validation, arrays, and quoted expansion without word splitting or glob injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A report script receives filenames "north 6.csv" and "--dry-run" plus one missing file. Preserve argument boundaries, validate before mutation, clean temporary state, and prove rerunning is idempotent.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
